abstract:
The discussion method is an instructional strategy designed to foster scientific dialogue among students, allowing them to collaborate, synthesize opinions, and develop alternative solutions to complex problems. In the context of Islamic Religious Education (IRE), this approach seeks to deepen students' understanding of their spiritual and social responsibilities toward God, fellow humans, and the natural world. This study investigates the correlation between the implementation of the discussion method (Variable X) and student learning outcomes (Variable Y) at MAN 1 Tangerang.

Drawing from a target population of 742 students, a sample of 38 eleventh-grade students was selected using non-probability quota sampling. Data were analyzed using the Pearson Product-Moment correlation formula. The analysis yielded a correlation coefficient ($r_{calculated}$) of 0.34, which exceeded the critical value ($r_{table}$) of 0.32 at a 5% significance level. Consequently, the null hypothesis—stating that the discussion method has no significant impact on learning outcomes—was rejected. These findings demonstrate that the discussion method exerts a statistically significant positive influence on student academic performance in IRE, particularly regarding the study of moral conduct (commendable and despicable traits) among eleventh-grade students at MAN 1 Tangerang.
